summary: Comprehensive E2E validation of the /create (Create Clone) flow on aiclonechats.com. 28/28 backend pytest cases pass (slug/display_name/bio/visibility validation, reserved slug, duplicate slug, IP/brand blocklist, avatar upload allow/reject, personality+topics persistence, happy path, clone limit=5). All 6 frontend Playwright scenarios pass (auth gate, form render, slug lowercase strip, happy path create + 'Clone created!' toast + /dashboard redirect, public clone page renders, Make-your-own button correctly routes to /create when signed in and /register?next=/create when anonymous). Two documented gaps: (1) celebrity/character names like 'Taylor Swift', 'Mickey Mouse' are NOT in _IP_BLOCKLIST_TERMS in clones.py — only studios/franchises/brand-bot names are. They may still be caught by the LLM safety filter but that's best-effort. (2) /api/clones POST has NO guard_expensive_action wrapper — 6 rapid creates never produced a 429 (only 400 'Clone limit reached' after 5). Both flagged as P2 backlog per task instructions, did NOT fail the suite.

minor.issue: No anti-abuse rate limit. payment.create_order has 5/min cap; /clones POST does not. 6 rapid creates only hit clone_limit (400), never rate_limited (429). P2 backlog.

_IP_BLOCKLIST_TERMS lacks celebrity names ('Taylor Swift', 'Mickey Mouse', 'Pikachu'). 'pokemon', 'disney' etc. are blocked, but bare celebrity names rely solely on LLM safety filter. Recommend extending the regex with a curated celebrity/character allowlist OR rename detail.code to clarify the boundary.

issues: No inline character counter on bio (max 400) — only enforced by maxLength=400 attribute; user gets no live feedback (MEDIUM, by-design per task brief)
No live slug-format hint as user types (uppercase silently stripped — fine, but no helper text saying so)
Empty display_name relies on HTML5 'required'; backend 422 toast never reached because browser native validation blocks submit

action_items: P2: Wrap POST /api/clones with guard_expensive_action (e.g., 5/min, 30/hour per user, admin bypass) to match the anti-abuse posture of payments.
P2: Extend _IP_BLOCKLIST_TERMS in /app/backend/clones.py with bare celebrity names (taylor swift, mickey mouse, pikachu, mario, etc.) OR document explicitly that celebrity blocking is LLM-side only.
P3: Consider adding inline character counter and live slug-format hint in CloneEditor.jsx (currently toast-only on submit — by design but a UX nicety).
critical_code_review_comments: clones.py:78 — POST /api/clones missing guard_expensive_action import + wrapper (rate-limit gap).

clones.py:21 _IP_BLOCKLIST_TERMS is studio/brand-only; user-facing copy in detail.message says 'brand, franchise, or trademark' which is accurate but the test brief expected celebrity blocking — mismatch.
models.py:61 CloneCreate.slug pattern ^[a-z0-9-]+$ correctly rejects uppercase/special; CloneEditor.jsx:184 also strips client-side — good defense-in-depth.
CloneEditor.jsx:83 frontend max 5MB cap matches storage.py:21 MAX_BYTES — consistent. 6MB returns 400 'File too large (max 5MB)' as expected.
seed_data_creation: Created and deleted ~15 throwaway clones during run; final cleanup test deletes all sr-tester clones. Confirmed 0 residue after run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend coverage for the create-clone flow is exhaustive in /app/backend/tests/test_clone_create_e2e.py with autouse fixture cleanup before each create to avoid hitting the 5-clone limit. Make-your-own button (data-testid=public-clone-make-your-own) works correctly in both signed-in (/create) and signed-out (/register?next=/create) states. Two known P2 gaps: (a) no rate limit on POST /api/clones, (b) celebrity-name IP blocklist not present at regex layer.
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional bugs found. Two enhancement gaps documented above.
